findings.evidence: `rg -n "groupLike.*groupLikeSetSpan|groupLikeSetSpan.*mono|mem_groupLikeSetSpan" ./code/TauCeti/Algebra/Coalgebra` finds only the definition and the raw membership restatement at lines 29 and 63.
findings.fix: Either make `groupLikeSetSpan` private if it is only an implementation helper for `groupLikeSpan`, or add characteristic API such as `groupLike_mem_groupLikeSetSpan`, `groupLikeSetSpan_le`, and `groupLikeSetSpan_mono`.
findings.issue: `groupLikeSetSpan` is public but has no span universal property or monotonicity theorem, so users cannot prove `groupLikeSetSpan s ≤ D` from generator membership without unfolding to `Submodule.span`.

findings.evidence: Lines 63 and 89 define the two `mem_` iff lemmas without attributes; `rg -n "@\[.*simp.*mem_groupLike(Set)?Span|mem_groupLike(Set)?Span" ./code/TauCeti/Algebra/Coalgebra/Subcoalgebra/GroupLike.lean` shows no simp annotation on either.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` to the membership normal-form lemmas, or replace them with simp-normal `*_iff` lemmas if those names better match the local API style.
findings.issue: The characteristic membership restatements `mem_groupLikeSetSpan` and `mem_groupLikeSpan` are not marked `@[simp]`, unlike the analogous `mem_ofSubmodule`, `mem_top`, and `mem_bot` API.

summary: The core subcoalgebra order API is now present, but the newly public group-like span constructor is not characterized enough to use as a public span object without unfolding through its submodule carrier.
